title: Statement Regarding the Publication of Reference Rates on Friday, April 3, 2026

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York announced that TGCR, BGCR, SOFR, and SOFR Averages and Index will not be published on Friday, April 3, 2026, due to cleared Treasury repo settlement not being scheduled for that date. The EFFR and OBFR, which follow the NY Fed's holiday schedule, will publish normally on April 3. On Monday, April 6, SOFR and related rates will resume publication.
key_points: TGCR, BGCR, SOFR, SOFR Averages and Index will not publish on April 3, 2026 due to cleared Treasury repo settlement unavailability
EFFR and OBFR follow the New York Fed's holiday schedule and will publish on April 3, 2026
SIFMA did not recommend a full closure of secondary market trading for U.S. government securities on April 3
Repo market participants broadly expect to treat April 3 as a holiday
Settlement of cleared Treasury repo is not scheduled for April 3, eliminating two of three market segments used to calculate Treasury repo reference rates
On April 6, TGCR, BGCR, and SOFR will publish reflecting same-day settling transactions that settle Thursday April 2 and mature Monday April 6
why_it_matters: This notice is critical for any market participant using SOFR or related overnight reference rates in derivatives pricing, loan agreements, or risk calculations. The one-day gap in SOFR publication affects daily accrual calculations, fallback provisions, and any contracts tied to these benchmarks. The differential treatment of EFFR/OBFR (published) versus SOFR (not published) on the same day may create basis considerations.
market_structure_context: The notice directly addresses the plumbing of reference rate publication, specifically the interdependencies between cleared repo settlement availability and SOFR calculation. It clarifies that the loss of cleared Treasury repo settlement eliminates two of three market segments used in Treasury repo reference rate calculations (TGCR and BGCR). The EFFR and OBFR are explicitly noted to follow a different publication schedule (NY Fed holiday schedule) than SOFR.

issues_found: source.url is empty - URL was not captured in crawl metadata. Domain (newyorkfed.org) was inferred but full URL is missing, violating source attribution completeness requirement.

summary states rates won't publish 'due to Good Friday and the March Employment Situation report' - this conflates the proximate cause. The source indicates the non-publication is due to cleared Treasury repo settlement unavailability; Good Friday and the employment report are upstream factors that caused that settlement issue. More precise phrasing would be 'due to cleared Treasury repo settlement not being scheduled for April 3.'

notes: This is an operational market notice, not a research archive piece. It provides factual information about reference rate publication schedule changes for a specific holiday date. The content is thin but authoritative. Source name and URL not captured in crawl metadata; populated with domain inference.
